articles.title: A Simple Procedure to Help Keep Weight Off After Stopping Medicine
articles.summary: Many people take medicines like Ozempic to lose weight. But when they stop taking the medicine, they often gain the weight back. A new study shows that a simple procedure might help people keep the weight off. The procedure is called duodenal mucosal resurfacing. It uses heat to fix the inside of the small intestine. This helps the body stay healthy and not gain back the weight. In the study, people who had the procedure gained back less weight than people who did not have it. The procedure is safe and people can go back to their normal activities in about one day. This could help many people who want to keep their weight off without taking medicine forever.

articles.title: New Brain-Like Chip Saves Energy
articles.summary: Scientists made a new computer chip that works like a human brain. Normal computers use a lot of electricity because they move data between memory and processing parts. This new chip does both at the same time, like our brain does. It can use 70% less energy. The chip is made from a special material called hafnium oxide. It has tiny parts called memristors that act like brain cells. They connect and talk to each other without using much power. In tests, the chip was very stable and could learn new things. This could help make future computers faster and better for the environment.

articles.title: Hidden Forces Inside Earth
articles.summary: Deep inside Earth, there is a layer called the mantle. It moves very slowly like honey. Scientists found that this movement can stretch and twist the rocks. They used earthquake waves to see inside Earth. When earthquakes happen, waves travel through Earth. The waves move differently depending on the rocks they go through. By studying these waves, scientists made a map of the deep mantle. They saw that most of the twisting happens where old pieces of Earth's crust have sunk down. This helps us understand how Earth's inside works.

articles.title: A Special Fat That Might Help Old Eyes See Better
articles.summary: As people get older, their eyes often don't work as well. Reading a menu in a dark restaurant can be hard. Scientists at the University of California, Irvine are studying a way to help old eyes see better again. They found that a special kind of fat, called a fatty acid, might help. In their study, they gave this fatty acid to old mice, and the mice's eyes got better. The scientists hope this could one day help people too. The special fat is not the same as omega-3, which is found in fish. The scientists think this new fat can reverse some of the aging in the eye. This is exciting because many older people have trouble seeing, and this could be a new treatment.

articles.title: A Call to Ban 'Forever Chemicals' in School Uniforms and Frying Pans
articles.summary: Some chemicals called PFAS are used to make things like school uniforms and frying pans waterproof and non-stick. But these chemicals are called 'forever chemicals' because they stay in the environment for a very long time and can be harmful to people and animals. A group of MPs in the UK wants to ban these chemicals unless companies can prove they are really needed. Many scientists and environmental groups are happy about this idea. The government says it will think about the suggestion. PFAS are found in many everyday items, like raincoats and medical equipment. They are useful because they resist water, oil, and heat. But they don't break down easily and can build up in our bodies. Some PFAS are known to cause health problems like cancer. The committee says we should stop using them in things like cookware and clothing by 2027, unless they are essential for safety. This is to protect our health and the environment.

articles.title: Scientists Solve a Magnet Mystery
articles.summary: Scientists have been studying tiny particles called muons. Muons are like electrons but heavier. They have a small magnet inside them. When muons are put in a magnetic field, they wobble. Scientists measured this wobble and thought it was different from what they expected. This difference suggested there might be a new force in nature. But a new study shows the difference was just a mistake in calculations. The old rules of physics still work. The scientists used a new method to check their numbers and found no new force. This means our understanding of the universe is still correct. The study helps us trust what we know about particles and forces.
